sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, presents with a non-healing foot ulcer on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, which has been present for approximately two months.
The ulcer has recently become red and is draining, causing concern for the patient.
The patient reports that he may have gotten the ulcer from walking barefoot on the beach at Dewey Beach.
The patient denies any specific pain, but reports occasional throbbing and increased pressure when touching the area.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes, which is not well-controlled, with an A1c of around 10%.
The patient takes medication for diabetes but does not regularly check his sugar levels.
The patient has not seen his primary care physician (PCP) recently and has not picked up the prescribed antibiotics.
Review of systems: The patient reports occasional throbbing and increased pressure in the foot, but no specific pain.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal, with no fever.
Physical exam: The patient has a one by two inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint.
The wound is red, draining, and has some edema around it.
The patient reports increased pressure when touching the area, but no distinct pain.
Test Results: The patient had an X-ray of the right foot, which showed no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with an infection, which is not well-controlled.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; Infection; Poorly controlled diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed clindamycin 400mg twice daily for seven days to treat the infection.
I will perform a debridement to remove dead tissue from the wound.
I will order an ABI (ankle-brachial index) to assess blood supply to the foot.
I will provide the patient with a surgical shoe to wear while the ulcer is healing.
The patient will follow up in two weeks for another debridement and assessment of the wound.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in two weeks for another debridement and assessment of the wound.
If the patient experiences any issues between appointments, he is instructed to call the office.
